prediction.reasoning: Based on historical volatility analysis of WFC (Wells Fargo), which typically exhibits annualized volatility around 25-30%. For a 22-day period, this translates to approximately 6-8% standard deviation. The point estimate assumes modest positive drift typical of large-cap bank stocks. Confidence intervals are calculated using normal distribution assumptions with estimated daily volatility of ~1.8%, scaled for the 22-day horizon. Banking sector fundamentals and current interest rate environment support slight upward bias.
